landing.heroLead: Lo strumento definitivo per ricerca e apprendimento. Carica qualsiasi PDF e guarda la magia: l'AI estrae ogni concetto, relazione e gerarchia trasformandoli in una mappa mentale interattiva e meravigliosa. Poi cerca in tutto ciò che hai mappato. Tutto sul tuo dispositivo. Zero cloud.
landing.philosophyQuote: Pensa al percorso che hai già compiuto. Ogni azione, ogni intuizione, ogni piccolo passo ti ha condotto qui. Marvex Studio non ti chiede di scegliere una nuova direzione — è la naturale continuazione della traiettoria che hai intrapreso. Il progresso sembra senza sforzo quando la nostra tecnologia avanzata si allinea con la tua storia, le tue sfide e le tue ambizioni. Fermarti ora contraddirebbe il percorso che hai già scelto come tuo.

magicMap.title: Magic Map
magicMap.body: Carica un PDF. In meno di un minuto, Claude Sonnet 4.5 estrae ogni concetto chiave, relazione e gerarchia, trasformandoli in una mappa interattiva meravigliosa che puoi zoomare, spostare ed esplorare.

secondBrain.title: Ogni mappa · Una ricerca
secondBrain.body: Una barra di ricerca per controllarle tutte. Interroga ogni mappa mentale che hai mai creato. Scopri connessioni tra articoli di cui non sapevi l'esistenza — anche dopo sei mesi.

localFirst.title: La tua chiave · Zero cloud
localFirst.body: Porta la tua chiave AI — la tua chiave, il tuo conto, i tuoi dati. Le mappe vivono nel tuo browser. Nessun account. Nessun tracciamento. Nessun raccolta di dati. Supporto offline completo.

cloudPush.title: Carica su Drive · Dropbox · Zotero
cloudPush.body: Un clic per duplicare una mappa su Google Drive, Dropbox o nella tua libreria Zotero — come PDF, PNG, SVG, Markdown o allegato binario completo. Tu scegli, ogni volta.

smartReader.title: Lettore PDF integrato
smartReader.body: Apri qualsiasi link PDF da un nodo e il Lettore integrato prende il controllo — evidenzia passaggi, annota i margini e invia le selezioni direttamente alla mappa che stai costruendo. Le tue fonti, le tue annotazioni, tutto sotto lo stesso tetto.

openAnything.title: Apri qualsiasi cosa · nel modo giusto
openAnything.body: Allega qualsiasi file o URL a un nodo. I PDF si aprono nel Lettore. Le canzoni si avviano nella tua app musicale, i video nel tuo player, i siti web nel browser predefinito — ogni link rispetta il modo in cui il tuo sistema operativo ama già lavorare.

calendar.title: Promemoria su un vero calendario
calendar.body: Assegna una data a un nodo e apparirà in una splendida vista calendario — tutte le scadenze, gli appuntamenti e i follow-up di ogni mappa in un unico posto. Locale, privato, nessun account Google richiesto.

bookmarks.title: Segnalibri Chrome → lista di lettura
bookmarks.body: Esporta i tuoi segnalibri Chrome una volta e Marvex trasforma l'intera gerarchia di cartelle in una mappa navigabile. Anni di link salvati, finalmente organizzati.

compileToPdf.title: Mappe → Documenti rifiniti
compileToPdf.body: L'inverso di PDF→Mappa. Scegli un nodo, un sotto-albero o l'intera mappa; l'AI lo trasforma in un saggio coerente, un briefing o uno schema. Esporta il risultato come PDF o Markdown — la tua mappa diventa una bozza pubblicabile, senza copia-incolla.

one.title: Carica il tuo PDF
one.body: Trascina qualsiasi paper, libro di testo o documento. Fino a 25 MB.

two.title: Succede la magia
two.body: In meno di 60 secondi, un generatore di mappa mentale AI estrae ogni concetto, tema e relazione in una mappa visiva.

three.title: Modifica ed esplora
three.body: Trascina i nodi, modifica i titoli, aggiungi rami, zooma e sposta. La tua mappa si salva automaticamente in locale.

landing.desktopLead: Apri lo studio e inizia a mappare. Si apre istantaneamente, funziona offline e non invia mai un byte della tua ricerca dove non dovrebbe.

footer.copyright: © 2026 marvex.app · Il Laboratorio di Ricerca Definitivo
footer.affiliateDisclosure: Marvex Studio partecipa al Programma Associati di Amazon Services LLC e al programma di affiliazione di Bookshop.org. I link ai libri potrebbero farci guadagnare una commissione senza costi aggiuntivi per te.

userBadge.manageSubscriptionHint: Manage subscription, update card, or cancel
userBadge.portalUnavailable: Customer portal unavailable. Email support@marvex.app and we'll handle it within 24h.

empty.title: La tua tela ti aspetta.
empty.body: Inizia con una mappa vuota, oppure carica un PDF e lascia che Claude lo trasformi in una mappa mentale strutturata e meravigliosa in pochi secondi.

modals.searchPlaceholder: Cerca in ogni mappa, ogni nodo…

todo.title: Le tue note adesive
todo.emptyBody: Nessuna nota adesiva ancora. Inseriscine una dalla barra degli strumenti per iniziare la tua lista.

index.title: Master Marvex Studio in minutes.
index.lead: Three short, hands-on tutorials that take you from a blank canvas to a full research workflow. No fluff, no filler — just the moves that matter.
index.takeInteractiveTourHint: A 60-second spotlight walk-through inside the studio.

welcome.title: Welcome to Marvex Studio
welcome.body: This quick 60-second tour highlights the four things you'll use most. Skip it at any time and return later from the Learn page.

sidebar.title: Your launchpad
sidebar.body: Start a New map, Open an existing one, or jump into PDF Studio, Library, and the Reader. Every tool you need lives in this sidebar.

canvas.title: The canvas
canvas.body: Drag, zoom, and pan freely. Double-click a node to rename it. Press Tab to add a child, Enter to add a sibling. Right-click for more options.

toolbar.title: Toolbar
toolbar.body: Undo · Redo · Shape · Export · Screenshot · Cloud Save. Hover any icon for its tooltip, or press ? any time for the full cheat-sheet.

share.title: Save & share
share.body: No save button — every change auto-saves locally. Click Share to copy a read-only link, or use Cloud Save (Pro) to push to Drive, Dropbox, or Zotero.

powerMoves.title: Bonus: 3 power moves
powerMoves.body: Shift-click two-or-more nodes, right-click, and pick 'Group as branch' to pull scattered ideas under one parent — or 'Join with line/arrow' to connect them visually. The 3-minute tutorial below shows it end-to-end.

firstMap.title: Create your first mind map
firstMap.subtitle: From blank canvas to a finished mind map in under five minutes.
firstMap.intro: Every mind-map in this app starts from a single central node. In this walk-through you'll create a new map, add branches, customise their shape and colour, and save it — all using just a few keyboard shortcuts. By the end you'll know every move you need to run Marvex Studio day-to-day.

step1.heading: Step 1 — Open the Studio
step1.body: Click the Marvex Studio button in the left sidebar, or press ⌘/Ctrl + N to create a brand new map. The canvas fills the screen and your cursor is already on the central node, waiting for you to rename it.

step2.heading: Step 2 — Rename the centre node
step2.body: Double-click the node to enter edit mode, type your topic, and press Enter. Short, punchy titles beat long sentences — this is the root of your tree so treat it like a chapter heading.

step3.heading: Step 3 — Add child branches
step3.body: Press Tab to spawn a new child under the selected node, or Enter to add a sibling. Hold Shift + Enter to add a sibling above. Keep going until your first ring of ideas is in place.

step4.heading: Step 4 — Style with right-click
step4.body: Right-click any node to open the context menu. Pick a shape (rectangle, ellipse, diamond, hexagon, cloud, star, pill), a fill colour from the palette, or switch to a bolder stroke. The cosmic presets match the rest of the app beautifully.

step5.heading: Step 5 — Pan, zoom, focus
step5.body: Hold Space + drag to pan the canvas. Scroll or pinch to zoom. Press F to frame the selected node. Press ? at any time to open the full keyboard cheat-sheet.

step6.heading: Step 6 — Save & share
step6.body: There's no save button — every change auto-saves locally the instant you make it. Click Share in the top bar to copy a read-only link, or use the Cloud Save toolbar button to push to Google Drive, Dropbox, or Zotero (Pro).

firstMap.tip: Pro tip: the Cmd/Ctrl + Z undo history holds 50 steps. You can experiment fearlessly.

pdfStudio.title: Turn a PDF into a mind map
pdfStudio.subtitle: Drop a paper, let Claude structure it, then refine the result by hand.
pdfStudio.intro: PDF Studio is the heart of Marvex Studio's research workflow. You hand it any research paper, textbook chapter, or document up to 25 MB, and an AI pass extracts every concept, theme, and relationship into a structured map you can edit. This tutorial covers the full pipeline — upload, pick a mode, review, refine, and merge with an existing map.

step1.heading: Step 1 — Open PDF Studio
step1.body: Click the purple PDF Studio button in the sidebar. You land on the Intake queue — a dashboard that tracks every document you've processed. Drag-drop a PDF onto the upload zone or click to browse.

step2.heading: Step 2 — Choose a conversion mode
step2.body: Quick Outline parses headings and structure heuristically — fast and free. AI Analysis (default) sends the full text to Claude Sonnet 4.5, which produces a semantic map rich with relationships and annotations. Pro users get unlimited AI runs; free users get three per month.

step3.heading: Step 3 — Review the draft
step3.body: Within 30-60 seconds the draft appears in the preview pane. Scroll through the node tree — every branch maps to a concept cluster from the paper, and every leaf carries a short summary. If a branch looks off, click it to flag it.

step4.heading: Step 4 — Fix & enrich
step4.body: Use the inline edit tools to rename, merge, split, or delete nodes. Right-click any node and pick 'Enrich with AI' to ask Claude for a deeper gloss, an example, or a contrary perspective. The original PDF stays pinned alongside the map so you can cross-reference quickly.

step5.heading: Step 5 — Commit to a map
step5.body: Once the draft looks right, click Save as New Map or Merge Into Existing. Merging respects your existing structure and inserts the new concepts as a fresh sub-tree you can reorganise later.

step6.heading: Step 6 — Skim with the Reader
step6.body: Open the Reader from the sidebar to read the PDF side-by-side with its map. Highlight any phrase in the PDF and Marvex Studio will automatically add it to the map as a citation node — perfect for building up evidence trails across papers.

pdfStudio.tip: Pro tip: batch-upload up to ten PDFs at once and the queue will process them in parallel.

aiResearch.title: Use the AI Research Assistant
aiResearch.subtitle: Ask any question across every map you've made. Get a grounded answer with citations.
aiResearch.intro: Your Second Brain isn't just a pretty tree — it's a searchable knowledge base. The AI Research Assistant pulls from every map, PDF, and highlight you've ever made, then answers your question with Claude Sonnet 4.5 grounded in that corpus. This tutorial covers asking questions, following citations, saving research notes, and Deep Research mode.

step1.heading: Step 1 — Open Research
step1.body: Press ⌘/Ctrl + K from anywhere in the app, or click the magnifying glass in the sidebar. The Research panel slides in from the right with an empty question box.

step2.heading: Step 2 — Ask in plain language
step2.body: Type a specific question. 'What does Popper say about falsifiability?' beats 'Popper'. The more precise your prompt, the tighter the citations. Press Enter to submit.

step3.heading: Step 3 — Read the streaming answer
step3.body: Claude streams the response token-by-token. Every claim comes with a citation chip — hover to see the source snippet, click to jump to the exact node or PDF highlight it's drawn from. Nothing is invented — if the corpus doesn't know, the assistant says so.

step4.heading: Step 4 — Toggle Deep Research
step4.body: For complex questions flip the Deep Research switch in the panel header. The assistant chains multiple searches, explores relationships, and builds a longer, better-structured answer. It takes longer but handles multi-step questions beautifully.

step5.heading: Step 5 — Save the response
step5.body: Click 'Save to Memory' to pin the answer — it becomes a research note accessible from the /memory page. Subsequent questions automatically consider your saved notes as context, so your assistant gets smarter the more you use it.

step6.heading: Step 6 — Drop into a map
step6.body: Every paragraph of the answer has a quick-add button. Click it to insert that paragraph as a new node on your current map, with the citation preserved as a sub-note. Your map grows organically alongside the conversation.

aiResearch.tip: Pro tip: ask follow-up questions in the same session to keep Claude's context. Each new session starts fresh.

reorganise.title: Reorganise a sprawling map
reorganise.subtitle: Pull scattered ideas under a parent branch in two clicks. No deleting, no copy-paste.
reorganise.intro: Maps grow fast. After a research session you'll often end up with eight or ten loose branches that obviously belong together — but moving them one-by-one is tedious. This walk-through shows the fastest path: shift-click to multi-select, right-click to group, rename. Same trick works for joining ideas with arrows or lines.

step1.heading: Step 1 — Spot the loose ideas
step1.body: Open any map that's gotten unwieldy. Look for two-or-more nodes that share a theme but live on different branches — symptoms, side-effects, sources, you name it. These are your join candidates.

step2.heading: Step 2 — Hold Shift and click each one
step2.body: Press and hold Shift, then click each candidate node. Every node you click stays in the pool — you'll see a fuchsia dashed outline around it. Release Shift when you're done. Plain click on any node clears the pool, so keep Shift held until the last one.

step3.heading: Step 3 — Right-click anywhere on the canvas
step3.body: With your pool selected, right-click on any empty canvas area (or on one of the highlighted nodes). The first three options are 'Join N with line', 'Join N with arrow', and 'Group N as branch' — pick whichever matches your intent.

step4.heading: Step 4 — Group as branch
step4.body: 'Group N as branch' detaches each selected sub-tree (with its descendants intact) and re-attaches them under a brand-new parent node called 'New group' — anchored to your map root. You're dropped straight into rename mode so you can give the branch a meaningful name and press Enter.

step5.heading: Step 5 — Or join with a line/arrow instead
step5.body: If the ideas should connect without changing the tree, pick 'Join with line' (or arrow). A free-floating connector appears between consecutive selections — drag the endpoints later, recolour from the right-click menu, or delete if you change your mind. Lines work between nodes, stickies, clipart, anything.

step6.heading: Step 6 — Compile it into a written brief (bonus)
step6.body: Once the branch is clean, click the scroll icon in the top toolbar (or right-click the new parent → Compile to document). The AI weaves every node title and summary into a 1-page brief, 3-page report, or 8-page deep-dive — your pick. Save it as PDF straight from the preview, or Copy MD into Notion. The fastest way to turn a brainstorm into a deliverable.

reorganise.tip: Pro tip: select 4+ items and 'Group as branch' will pull them all under one parent in a single click — perfect for mid-PDF pivots when you realise three sections are really one super-topic.
